- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# MHPCO Claim Office — Example Mapping
2	
3	Shared specification memory for the `red`/`green` subagents. This file
4	condenses the requirements from `prompt.md`. Use the exact field names
5	from the Interface Contract below.
6	
7	## Rules
8	
9	### R1. Item base values and premiums
10	
11	Main items (insurance value / base premium):
12	- `sword`: 1000 G / 100 G
13	- `amulet`: 600 G / 60 G
14	- `staff`: 800 G / 80 G
15	- `potion`: 400 G / 40 G
16	
17	Components (`rune`, `moonstone`, ...): 250 G insurance value, 25 G base
18	premium per component. **Building block:** exactly 3 alike components
19	(same `type` — `rune` ≠ `moonstone`) get a special base premium of 60 G
20	instead of 3 × 25 = 75 G.
21	
22	- 2 of a type → 2 × 25 = 50 G
23	- 3 of a type → 60 G (block applies)
24	- 4 of a type → 4 × 25 = 100 G (block requires exactly 3)
25	- 7 of a type → 7 × 25 = 175 G
26	
27	Multiple component types are counted independently:
28	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 50 + 25 = 75 G
29	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 60 + 60 = 120 G (two blocks)
30	
31	### R2. Premium modifiers
32	
33	Item-specific surcharges (apply to that item's base premium only):
34	- **Cursed** (`cursed: true`): +50 % of that item's base premium
35	- **High enchantment** (`enchantment >= 5`): +30 % of that item's base premium
36	
37	Policy-wide modifiers (apply to the **policy base premium** = sum of all
38	item base premiums + item-specific surcharges):
39	- **Loyalty** (customer `yearsWithMHPCO >= 2`): −20 %
40	- **First insurance**: +10 % — applies to **every item in every quote**,
41	  regardless of customer history. (Clarified: each item in a quote is
42	  treated as a first insurance.) Interpretation: this is a policy-wide
43	  +10 % surcharge on the policy base premium (applies once per quote).
44	- **Follow-up contract** (any quote after the customer's first quote in
45	  the scenario): −15 % of the policy base premium
46	
47	Final: +5 G processing fee added at the very end.
48	
49	Rounding: all amounts are rounded to **whole G in MHPCO's favor**:
50	- premiums round **up** (197.5 → 198)
51	- payouts round **down** (350.5 → 350)
52	- intermediate values kept as fractions; only the final premium/payout
53	  rounded.
54	
55	### R3. Claim processing
56	
57	- **Deductible**: 100 G applies **per damage event** (per entry in
58	  `damages`).
59	- **High-enchantment clause** (item's `enchantment >= 8`): reimburse 50 %
60	  of damage amount, **then** subtract deductible.
61	- **Dragon-material clause** (item's `material === "dragon"`): full
62	  reimbursement, then deductible.
63	- If both clauses apply, the 50 % clause wins (then deductible).
64	- If neither clause applies: full reimbursement minus deductible.
65	
66	Components (`rune`, `moonstone`) have no `enchantment` or `material`, so
67	no special clause applies; treat as standard reimbursement.
68	
69	### R4. Multiple items of same type
70	
71	- A quote may contain multiple items of the same type (e.g. two swords).
72	  Insurance sum stacks (2 swords → 2000 G).
73	- A claim's `damages` array may contain multiple entries of the same
74	  `itemType`; each entry is a separate damage event with its own
75	  deductible.
76	- If `damages` contains more entries of a given itemType than the policy
77	  covers (e.g. 2 sword damages but only 1 sword insured), the **whole
78	  claim is rejected**: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr, no result.
79	
80	### R5. Edge cases / errors (CLI exits non-zero, error on stderr)
81	
82	- Quote with empty `items` array → premium 5 G (only the fee). Not an
83	  error.
84	- Quote contains item with unknown type (e.g. `broomstick`) → reject:
85	  CLI exits non-zero, error on stderr, no `results` written to stdout.
86	- Claim references a damage entry for an item not in the policy
87	  (e.g. amulet damage but only a sword insured, or unknown itemType) →
88	  CLI exits non-zero, error on stderr.
89	- Claim contains a damage entry with negative `amount` (e.g. `-200`) →
90	  CLI exits non-zero, error on stderr.
91	
92	### R6. Scenario semantics
93	
94	- One `customer` for the whole scenario, with `yearsWithMHPCO`.
95	- `steps` is an ordered array. Each step has `op: "quote"` or
96	  `op: "claim"`.
97	- `claim` step has a `policy` field with the **zero-based index** of the
98	  `quote` step that created the policy.
99	- Output `results` has the same length and order as input `steps`.
100	- "Follow-up contract" −15 % discount applies to **every quote after the
101	  customer's first quote within this scenario** (claim steps don't
102	  count toward this counter).
103	
104	## Examples
105	
106	### E1. Building block of 3 alike components (R1)
107	- 2 runes → base 50 G
108	- 3 runes → base 60 G
109	- 4 runes → base 100 G
110	- 7 runes → base 175 G
111	
112	### E2. Alike means same exact type (R1)
113	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 75 G base
114	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120 G base
115	
116	### E3. Modifier scope — cursed surcharge applies per-item (R2)
117	- Cursed sword (100 G base) + plain amulet (60 G base):
118	  - policy base = 160 G; cursed surcharge = 50 G (50% of sword);
119	    sum = 210 G *before* policy-wide modifiers and fee.
120	
121	### E4. Modifier thresholds (R2/R3)
122	- Exactly 2 years → loyalty discount applies
123	- Exactly enchantment 5 → high-enchantment surcharge applies; both
124	  apply if also cursed
125	- Enchantment 4 → no high-enchantment surcharge
126	- Dragon-material sword, enchantment exactly 8, damage 1000 G → payout
127	  400 G (high-enchantment clause kicks in: 1000 × 0.5 = 500, − 100
128	  deductible = 400)
129	
130	### E5. Deductible per damage event (R3)
131	- Dragon attack on sword (damage 500) + amulet (damage 300):
132	  payout = (500−100) + (300−100) = 600 G.
133	
134	### E6. Standard reimbursement (R3)
135	- Regular sword (steel, enchantment 3), damage 500 → 400 G payout
136	- Rune damage (insurance 250), damage 200 → 100 G payout
137	
138	### E7. Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material (R3)
139	- Dragon-material sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 → 400 G
140	- Dragon-material sword, enchantment 5, damage 800 → 700 G
141	- Steel sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 → 400 G
142	
143	### E8. Multiple items of same type (R4)
144	- 2 swords in a quote → insurance sum 2000 G
145	- 2 damage entries of `sword` against a 2-sword policy: each separately
146	  reimbursed (each with own deductible)
147	- 2 sword damages, only 1 sword insured → whole claim rejected (CLI
148	  exits non-zero)
149	
150	### E9. Rounding in MHPCO's favor (R2)
151	- 197.5 G premium → 198 G (up)
152	- 350.5 G payout → 350 G (down)
153	- Intermediates stay fractional.
154	
155	### E10. Edge cases (R5)
156	- Empty items → premium 5 G
157	- Unknown item type (`broomstick`) → error
158	- Claim for item not in policy → error
159	- Negative damage amount → error
160	
161	### E11. Integration — newcomer with cursed sword
162	- Customer: `yearsWithMHPCO: 0`, no previous contract
163	- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 3)
164	- Calculation: 100 base + 50 curse = 150; +10 first insurance = 160;
165	  +5 fee = **165 G**
166	
167	### E12. Integration — long-standing customer's second contract
168	- Customer: `yearsWithMHPCO: 3`; second quote in scenario
169	- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7)
170	- Calculation: base 100; +50 curse; +30 high enchantment = 180 item
171	  total. Policy-wide: −20 loyalty (−20 % of 180 = −36? but spec says
172	  "−20 G loyalty"). The spec's worked breakdown is:
173	  `100 base + 50 curse + 30 high-ench − 20 loyalty + 10 first insurance
174	  − 15 follow-up = 155 + 5 fee = 160`.
175	- This means policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance,
176	  follow-up) are computed as percentages of the **base premium of
177	  100 G** (not the post-surcharge total): 20 % of 100 = 20, 10 % of
178	  100 = 10, 15 % of 100 = 15. This matches the example values
179	  exactly. (Item-specific surcharges are added separately and are not
180	  themselves subject to policy-wide discounts.)
181	- **Final formula:**
182	  premium = sum_items(base + item_surcharges)
183	          + policy_base × (first_insurance + loyalty + follow_up)
184	          + processing_fee
185	  where policy_base = sum of item base premiums (no surcharges), and
186	  the policy-wide modifiers are signed percentages.
187	- Sanity-check with E3: cursed sword (base 100) + plain amulet
188	  (base 60). policy_base = 160. Item surcharges = 50 (curse). Sum =
189	  210 G "before further modifiers and fee" ✓.
190	- Sanity-check with E11: cursed sword only. policy_base = 100.
191	  Item surcharges = 50 (curse). Policy-wide: +10 first insurance
192	  = 10 % × 100 = 10 G. Total: 100 + 50 + 10 = 160; +5 fee = 165 G ✓.
193	
194	## Interface Contract
195	
196	The CLI `claim-office` (entry point `src/cli.ts`) reads JSON from stdin
197	and writes JSON to stdout. Field names below are **binding**.
198	
199	### Stdin (scenario)
200	
201	```json
202	{
203	  "customer": { "yearsWithMHPCO": 5 },
204	  "steps": [
205	    {
206	      "op": "quote",
207	      "items": [
208	        { "type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false }
209	      ]
210	    },
211	    {
212	      "op": "claim",
213	      "policy": 0,
214	      "incident": {
215	        "cause": "fire",
216	        "damages": [
217	          { "itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200 }
218	        ]
219	      }
220	    }
221	  ]
222	}
223	```
224	
225	- `customer.yearsWithMHPCO`: integer.
226	- `steps[].op`: `"quote"` or `"claim"`.
227	- Quote step: `items[]` with `type` (string: `sword`/`amulet`/`staff`/
228	  `potion`/`rune`/`moonstone`). Optional: `material`, `enchantment`,
229	  `cursed`.
230	- Claim step: `policy` (zero-based step index of the quote that created
231	  the policy); `incident.cause` (string), `incident.damages[]` with
232	  `itemType` and `amount` (integer G).
233	
234	### Stdout (results)
235	
236	```json
237	{ "results": [ { "premium": 71 }, { "payout": 100 } ] }
238	```
239	
240	- `results[]` same length and order as `steps[]`.
241	- Quote result: `{ "premium": <integer G> }`.
242	- Claim result: `{ "payout": <integer G> }`.
243	
244	### Error behavior
245	
246	On any of the error cases listed in R5, the CLI:
247	- Exits with a **non-zero** status code.
248	- Writes a human-readable error description to **stderr**.
249	- Does **not** write a partial `results` document to stdout.
250	
251	### Internal API for unit tests
252	
253	The implementation file `src/claim-office.ts` should expose a
254	synchronous function that runs a parsed scenario object and returns the
255	results object (or throws on invalid input). Suggested signature
256	(tests will pin this down progressively):
257	
258	```ts
259	export function runScenario(scenario: Scenario): { results: ResultEntry[] };
260	```
261	
262	Unit tests import `runScenario` (and possibly smaller helpers as they
263	emerge). CLI tests in `src/cli.spec.ts` (or via `src/claim-office.spec.ts`)
264	exercise the stdin/stdout/exit-code surface end-to-end by spawning the
265	CLI as a subprocess via `tsx src/cli.ts`.
266	
267	## Questions / Clarifications
268	
269	- ❓ "Alike" components — **same exact type** (rune ≠ moonstone). [E2]
270	- ❓ Modifier scope on multi-item policy — item-specific (curse, high
271	  enchantment) apply to that item's base premium only; policy-wide
272	  (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) apply to the **policy base
273	  premium** (sum of item base premiums, without item surcharges). [E3,
274	  E12]
275	- ❓ "First insurance" — applies to **each item in every quote**,
276	  regardless of customer history. Practically: every quote receives the
277	  +10 % first-insurance surcharge (on policy base premium). [E12]
278	- ❓ Multiple items of same type — allowed; each damage is a separate
279	  event; over-claiming is rejected outright. [E8]
280	
281	## Per-test Rationale (covered by `it.todo`s)
282	
283	The test list below covers all rules and examples. Numbering matches
284	the order in `src/claim-office.spec.ts`.
285	
286	Quote pricing (unit tests via `runScenario`):
287	1. Empty items quote → premium 5 G (E10/R5 — fee only).
288	2. Single plain amulet (yearsWithMHPCO 0) → base 60 + first 6 + fee 5 = 71 G (R1 + first insurance).
289	3. Single plain sword (newcomer) → 100 + 10 + 5 = 115 G (R1 + first insurance).
290	4. Newcomer with cursed sword (steel, ench 3) → 165 G (E11 integration).
291	5. Sword exactly enchantment 5 (newcomer, plain) → 100 + 30 high-ench + 10 first = 140 + 5 fee = 145 G (E4 threshold).
292	6. Sword enchantment 4 (newcomer, plain) → no high-ench: 100 + 10 + 5 = 115 G (E4 threshold).
293	7. Cursed sword exactly enchantment 5 (newcomer) → 100 + 50 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 195 G (E4 both surcharges).
294	8. Long-standing customer (≥2 years), single plain sword, only quote → 100 + 10 first − 20 loyalty + 5 fee = 95 G (R2 loyalty + first insurance).
295	9. Customer with exactly 2 years (threshold) → loyalty applies, same calc as 8.
296	10. Long-standing customer's second contract: cursed sword ench 7 → 160 G (E12 integration).
297	11. Multi-item policy: cursed sword + plain amulet, newcomer → policy base 160 + 50 curse = 210; +16 first (10 % × 160) = 226; +5 fee = 231 G (E3 modifier scope; verifies first-insurance applies to policy base).
298	12. Components — 2 runes → base 50 + 5 first + 5 fee = 60 G (E1 block edge).
299	13. Components — 3 runes (block applies) → base 60 + 6 first + 5 fee = 71 G (E1 block applies).
300	14. Components — 4 runes (no block) → base 100 + 10 first + 5 fee = 115 G (E1).
301	15. Components — 7 runes → base 175 + 17.5 first = 192.5; +5 fee = 197.5 → round UP to 198 G (E1 + E9 rounding up).
302	16. Components — 2 runes + 1 moonstone → base 75 + 7.5 first + 5 fee = 87.5 → round up 88 G (E2 alike means same type).
303	17. Components — 3 runes + 3 moonstones (two blocks) → base 120 + 12 first + 5 fee = 137 G (E2 two blocks).
304	18. Two swords in one quote (newcomer) → base 200 + 20 first + 5 fee = 225 G (E8 stacking).
305	
306	Claim payouts:
307	19. Standard claim: steel sword ench 3, damage 500 → payout 400 G (E6).
308	20. Component damage: rune damage 200 → 100 G (E6 component standard).
309	21. High-enchantment-only: steel sword ench 9, damage 1000 → 400 G (E7).
310	22. Dragon-material only, ench 5, damage 800 → 700 G (E7).
311	23. Dragon-material AND ench 9, damage 1000 → 400 G (E7 50% wins).
312	24. Dragon-material sword exactly ench 8, damage 1000 → 400 G (E4 threshold).
313	25. Multiple damage entries: dragon attack on sword (damage 500) + amulet (damage 300) → 600 G (E5 deductible per event).
314	26. Two swords insured, claim with two sword damages (e.g. 400 + 300, steel ench 3) → 300 + 200 = 500 G (E8 each event independent).
315	27. Payout rounding: contrived damage yielding 350.5 → 350 G (E9 round DOWN). E.g. dragon-material sword ench 8, damage 901 → 901×0.5 − 100 = 350.5 → 350.
316	
317	Error / rejection cases (CLI surface — spawn `tsx src/cli.ts`):
318	28. CLI happy path: scenario stdin → results stdout, exit 0 (schema example).
319	29. CLI: unknown item type in quote (`broomstick`) → exit non-zero, error on stderr, no results on stdout (R5/E10).
320	30. CLI: claim references item not in policy (amulet damage on sword-only policy) → exit non-zero, error on stderr (R5/E10).
321	31. CLI: claim with unknown itemType → exit non-zero, error on stderr (R5/E10).
322	32. CLI: damage entry with negative amount → exit non-zero, error on stderr (R5/E10).
323	33. CLI: claim with more sword damages than swords insured → exit non-zero (E8 over-claim rejection).
324	34. CLI: full scenario with multiple quotes and a claim — follow-up discount applies to second quote; results array preserves order and step indexing. (R6 + E12 integration end-to-end.)
325
